stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant open-source LLM.

stories.title: Qualcomm promises $300 Windows laptops with new Snapdragon C
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Qualcomm is aggressively targeting the budget laptop market with its new Snapdragon C platform.
The company aims to bring Arm-based Windows laptops down to a price point of $300 this year.
This move signals a significant industry shift toward lower-cost, power-efficient computing options.

stories.title: Nvidia bets $150B on Taiwan as Trump's plan to make US an AI hub backfires
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Nvidia is committing a massive $150 billion annual investment to Taiwan.
The stated goal is to establish Taiwan as a global AI 'epicenter'.
The investment is framed against the backdrop of shifting US AI hub strategies under Trump's plans.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML domains like transformers and text-generation.
This indicates ongoing community interest and evaluation of Meta's latest LLM releases.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is focused on text generation, indicating generative AI relevance.
Key technologies mentioned include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, placing it in the core ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized local AI inference.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users through a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: CNN sues Perplexity over ‘verbatim’ copycat articles
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: CNN has sued Perplexity, alleging the AI tool generates 'verbatim' copies of its journalistic content.
The lawsuit also claims Perplexity is distributing information that is paywalled behind CNN's subscription.
This signals an emerging legal conflict regarding AI content generation and copyright infringement in the media industry.

stories.title: Temu fined more than $230 million by EU over illegal product sales
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Temu has been fined over €200 million ($232 million) by the European Commission.
The fine stems from the platform's failure to prevent consumers from encountering illegal items.
The breach violates the EU's Digital Services Act (DSA) regarding systemic risk assessment.

stories.title: Bad cholesterol slashed 62% by single dose of gene-editing drug in small trial
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: A gene-editing drug demonstrated a significant reduction (62%) in bad cholesterol.
The findings come from an interim Phase I clinical trial involving 35 participants.
The results are promising, suggesting a potential breakthrough in cardiovascular treatment.

stories.title: CubePart: An Open-Vocabulary Part-Controllable 3D Generator
stories.author: Tinghui Zhou
bullet_points: CubePart is a new generative framework for creating 3D meshes with open-vocabulary and part-controllable features.
It addresses a key limitation in current 3D generation models by explicitly exposing part structure for application-specific control.
The technology is highly relevant for industries like gaming and simulation, where semantic decomposition of assets is crucial for animation and physics.

stories.title: Bias Leaves a Gradient Trail: Label-Free Bias Identification via Gradient Probes on Concept Decompositions
stories.author: Jae Hee Lee
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, label-free method to identify spurious correlations (bias) in pre-trained vision models.
Addresses a critical limitation in current bias mitigation by operating post-hoc on frozen models without needing specific bias labels.
The technique uses 'gradient probes on concept decompositions' to detect biases that cause models to fail under distribution shift.

stories.title: Five frontier LLMs disagree on 67% of 1k real-world fact-check claims
stories.author: kostaj
bullet_points: Multiple frontier Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit significant disagreement on factual claims.
The disagreement rate was found to be high, with models disagreeing on 67% of 1,000 real-world fact-check claims.
This highlights current limitations in LLM consensus and factual reliability across different advanced models.

stories.title: YouTube to automatically label AI-generated videos
stories.author: nopg
bullet_points: YouTube is implementing automatic labeling for AI-generated videos.
This feature aims to increase transparency regarding synthetic media content on the platform.
The move signals a growing industry focus on combating misinformation and deepfakes.

stories.title: EU fines Temu €200M for allowing sale of illegal products
stories.author: jjp
bullet_points: The European Union has issued a substantial fine of €200 million against the e-commerce platform Temu.
The fine was levied due to Temu's alleged failure to prevent the sale of illegal products on its platform.
This highlights increasing regulatory scrutiny from the EU regarding online marketplaces and product safety.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is introducing experimental local image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for macOS users.
Support for Windows and Linux is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within Ollama.
It bridges the gap between open-source models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code in a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: These new iOS 27 renders hint at Siri’s big redesign
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: iOS 27 is rumored to feature a major redesign of Siri.
The new Siri interface is suggested to resemble ChatGPT, incorporating a 'Liquid Glass' aesthetic.
The information is based on renders provided by Bloomberg and internal sources, indicating an upcoming overhaul.

stories.title: Rivian’s software chief thinks you don’t need CarPlay or buttons
stories.author: Nilay Patel
bullet_points: Rivian's CSO, Wassym Bensaid, is advocating for a software-centric approach to vehicle control, potentially minimizing reliance on traditional interfaces like CarPlay or physical buttons.
Bensaid also co-leads RV Tech, a significant joint venture between Rivian and Volkswagen backed by a nearly $6 billion investment.
The discussion highlights the industry trend toward advanced, software-defined vehicle architectures.

stories.title: Intel’s first handheld gaming chip is the Arc G3, and this Acer is using it
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Intel is entering the handheld gaming PC market with its custom chip, the Arc G3.
This move signals a potential shift in Intel's strategy within the competitive handheld gaming space.
The Arc G3 is being integrated into a new handheld device from Acer.

stories.title: Websites have a new way to spy on visitors: Analyzing their SSD activity
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Websites can now monitor visitor activity by analyzing Solid State Drive (SSD) usage.
This surveillance capability can be implemented using straightforward JavaScript within the browser.
The development represents a new, subtle vector for tracking user behavior online.

stories.title: Mystery GPS jammer in Iran becomes test for NASA satellites’ capabilities
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: NASA science satellites are being utilized to locate sources of GPS interference.
The incident involves a mystery GPS jammer operating in Iran.
The story highlights the dual-use capabilities of advanced satellite technology.

stories.title: Beyond Binary: Sim-to-Real Dexterous Manipulation with Physics-Grounded Contact Representation
stories.author: Toru Lin
bullet_points: Addresses the 'sim-to-real' gap in dexterous manipulation, a major bottleneck in robotics.
Proposes a novel method, 'Center-of-Pressure,' to effectively use rich, high-dimensional tactile data in simulation.
Aims to overcome the limitation of existing methods that simplify complex touch data into coarse features.

stories.title: Calibrating Conservatism for Scalable Oversight
stories.author: Mohsen Bayati
bullet_points: Addresses the fundamental control problem of maintaining human oversight over highly capable, autonomous Agentic AI systems.
Proposes 'Calibrated Collective Oversight (CCO)' as a novel framework for scalable oversight.
CCO aggregates diverse auxiliary scoring functions to provide oversight with statistical guarantees, addressing limitations of existing heuristic methods.

stories.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration
stories.author: Ling Yang
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical need for reliable, fine-grained verification in multimodal large language models (LLMs) as visual data becomes central.
It introduces 'multimodal meta-verification,' which utilizes detailed rationales generated by verifiers instead of simple pass/fail decisions.
The work focuses on effectively incorporating this meta-verification feedback into the training process of multimodal verifiers.

stories.title: CaMBRAIN: Real-time, Continuous EEG Inference with Causal State Space Models
stories.author: Mubarak Shah
bullet_points: Introduces CaMBRAIN, a new approach for real-time, continuous EEG inference.
Addresses limitations of existing EEG deep learning models, specifically the quadratic scaling of attention mechanisms with long sequences.
Overcomes the constraint of fixed-length inputs by moving beyond traditional sliding-window processing for continuous EEG monitoring.

stories.title: Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation for LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Yuzhi Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation (SGSD) to enhance LLM reasoning.
Moves beyond traditional self-distillation by deriving privileged information (PI) from an experience-derived skill bank instead of trusted reference answers.
Addresses the challenge of using potentially irrelevant or misleading retrieved skills in the distillation process.

stories.title: Do Agents Need Semantic Metadata? A Comparative Study in Agentic Data Retrieval
stories.author: Natasha Noy
bullet_points: The paper investigates the necessity of traditional semantic metadata (like schema.org) in the context of autonomous AI agents.
It addresses the challenge posed by Large Language Models (LLMs) that can navigate unstructured web data, potentially bypassing the need for rigid metadata.
The research is relevant to the future of machine-actionable data and adherence to FAIR principles in agentic workflows.

stories.title: Rethinking Memory as Continuously Evolving Connectivity
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: Proposes FluxMem, a new memory framework for LLM agents that moves beyond static memory repositories.
Models memory as a heterogeneous graph whose topology is progressively refined based on dynamic interactions.
Addresses the brittleness of current memory-augmented LLMs in environments with continuous feedback and task variation.

stories.title: SwarmHarness: Skill-Based Task Routing via Decentralized Incentive-Aligned AI Agent Networks
stories.author: Edwin Jose
bullet_points: SwarmHarness proposes a decentralized protocol to utilize vast amounts of unused compute resources (GPUs, edge devices).
It addresses limitations in existing systems, such as reliance on central coordinators or heavy blockchain infrastructure.
The core innovation is an incentive-aligned AI agent network designed to facilitate safe and profitable resource sharing.

stories.title: PEFT-Arena: Understanding Parameter-Efficient Finetuning from a Stability-Plasticity Perspective
stories.author: Weiyang Liu
bullet_points: Introduces PEFT-Arena, a new benchmark for evaluating Parameter-Efficient Finetuning (PEFT) in LLMs.
Addresses a gap in current PEFT evaluations by focusing on the stability-plasticity trade-off.
Measures both downstream task performance and the retention of general pretrained capabilities.

stories.title: Beyond Binary: Sim-to-Real Dexterous Manipulation with Physics-Grounded Contact Representation
stories.author: Toru Lin
bullet_points: Addresses the sim-to-real gap in dexterous manipulation, a major bottleneck in robotics.
Proposes a novel 'Center-of-Pressure' contact representation to preserve rich tactile information, unlike existing coarse methods.
Improves upon current reinforcement learning approaches by enabling the effective use of high-fidelity touch data from simulations.

stories.title: Affective Music Recommendation: A Rollout-Based World Model for Offline Preference Optimization
stories.author: Laurent Charlin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of recommending music based on affective state (emotion) where online testing is ethically constrained.
Introduces AMRS, a Rollout-Based World Model designed for offline preference optimization in music recommendation.
The system is deployed on health-and-wellness platforms, targeting clinical users, highlighting a practical application in digital health.

stories.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration
stories.author: Ling Yang
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical need for reliable, fine-grained verification in multimodal large language models (LLMs).
It introduces 'OmniVerifier-M1,' a multimodal meta-verifier that utilizes generated rationales instead of simple decision signals for verification.
The work explores methods to effectively integrate meta-verification feedback into the training process of multimodal verifiers.

stories.title: Ω-QVLA: Robust Quantization for Vision-Language-Action Models via Composite Rotation and Per-step Scaling
stories.author: Peng Lu
bullet_points: The research addresses the high computational cost of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, which are crucial for unifying perception, reasoning, and control.
It proposes 'Ω-QVLA,' a new quantization method that tackles the deployment challenge by addressing the instability of quantizing diffusion-based action heads.
The technique utilizes 'Composite Rotation and Per-step Scaling' to achieve robust quantization across the entire VLA architecture.

stories.title: CaMBRAIN: Real-time, Continuous EEG Inference with Causal State Space Models
stories.author: Mubarak Shah
bullet_points: Addresses the scalability issue of deep learning models (like attention mechanisms) when analyzing long-duration EEG signals.
Introduces CaMBRAIN, a method utilizing Causal State Space Models for real-time, continuous EEG inference.
Overcomes the limitation of traditional sliding-window processing required by fixed-length input models for continuous brain monitoring.

stories.title: Rethinking Memory as Continuously Evolving Connectivity
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: Proposes FluxMem, a novel memory framework for LLM agents that moves beyond static memory storage.
Models memory as a heterogeneous graph whose topology is progressively refined based on dynamic environmental feedback.
Addresses the brittleness of current memory-augmented LLMs in complex, evolving agentic environments.

stories.title: PEFT-Arena: Understanding Parameter-Efficient Finetuning from a Stability-Plasticity Perspective
stories.author: Weiyang Liu
bullet_points: Introduces PEFT-Arena, a new benchmark for evaluating Parameter-Efficient Finetuning (PEFT) methods.
Addresses a gap in current PEFT evaluations by focusing on the stability-plasticity trade-off.
Measures both downstream task performance and the retention of the model's general pretrained capabilities.

stories.title: Self-Improving Language Models with Bidirectional Evolutionary Search
stories.author: Yilun Du
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in current self-improvement methods for language models (e.g., best-of-N sampling) which rely on sparse verification signals.
The proposed method, 'Bidirectional Evolutionary Search,' aims to overcome the restriction of autoregressive expansion by enabling broader exploration.
The research focuses on using search techniques for both post-training sample generation and improving inference capabilities in language models and agentic systems.

stories.title: Personal Visual Memory from Explicit and Implicit Evidence
stories.author: Yuheng Li
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI memory systems, which are predominantly text-centric, despite the growing need for personalized AI agents.
Highlights that images often contain crucial personal information (explicit and implicit) that is frequently lost or reduced to generic captions in existing models.
Proposes a method to leverage both explicit and implicit visual evidence for building more robust, personalized long-term memory in AI.

stories.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration
stories.author: Ling Yang
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical need for reliable, fine-grained verification in multimodal large language models (LLMs).
It introduces 'multimodal meta-verification,' which utilizes detailed rationales from verifiers instead of just binary decisions.
The work explores methods to effectively integrate this meta-verification feedback into the training process of multimodal verifiers.

stories.title: Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation for LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Yuzhi Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation (SGSD) to enhance LLM reasoning.
Moves beyond relying on trusted, static privileged information (PI) by using an experience-derived skill bank.
Addresses the challenge of using potentially irrelevant or misleading skills retrieved from the bank for supervision.

stories.title: The Abstraction Gap in Vision-Language Causal Reasoning
stories.author: Mohammad Rashedul Hasan
bullet_points: Addresses a critical limitation in Vision-Language Models (VLMs): distinguishing between fluent linguistic explanations and actual causal reasoning.
Proposes a novel dual-probe methodology (Text-Only and Chain-Text) to rigorously evaluate causal reasoning capabilities.
Introduces the Abstraction Gap (AG) metric to quantify the performance disparity between linguistic plausibility and causal faithfulness across eight VLMs.

stories.title: AMD pulls a bait-and-switch on Linux users with Vivado licensing changes
stories.author: teleforce
bullet_points: AMD has reportedly changed its Vivado licensing terms affecting Linux users.
The change is described by the source as a 'bait-and-switch' targeting the Linux community.
This indicates potential friction between hardware vendors (AMD) and open-source ecosystems.

stories.title: AI Agents Are Great at 80% of Our Code. The Other 20% Is Why We Still Need Seniors.
stories.author: mickyarun
bullet_points: AI agents demonstrated high proficiency (80%) in handling routine coding tasks on a payment platform.
The agents failed or introduced critical errors in the remaining 20% of complex code.
The article suggests that human senior expertise remains necessary for handling complex, edge-case scenarios in software development.

stories.title: Treat AI Coding Agents Like Untrusted Interns: A Practical Sandbox Checklist
stories.author: nimay_04
bullet_points: The article provides a practical checklist for managing AI coding agents.
It emphasizes the need to treat these agents with caution, similar to untrusted interns.
The core focus is on implementing disposable, least-privilege developer environments for safety.
